taxonomy: Trichodrymonia gigantea is classified within the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family, which comprises around 600 genera and 13,000 species of flowering plants. The Rubiaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse families in the order Gentianales. Within the family, Trichodrymonia gigantea is further classified under the tribe Vanguerieae, which is characterized by its unique floral and morphological characteristics. The tribe Vanguerieae is itself part of the subfamily Ixoroideae, which is a distinct lineage within the Rubiaceae family. Trichodrymonia gigantea is thus nested within a complex phylogenetic framework that reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants.
